LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER THOMAS BROWN'S MOTION FOR ISSUANCE OF PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE IS CONTINUED FROM JUN-14-2006 TO JUL-10-2006 ON COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR 8189
LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONER THOMAS BROWN FOR ISSUANCE OF PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE CONTINUED FROM JUL-10-2006 TO JUL-11-2006, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 301.

LAW AND MOTION 301, AFTER A PERSONAL DISCLOSURE OF THE COURT, THIS MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT GRANTED PETITIONER THOMAS BROWN'S PETITION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE. THE MATTER IS REMANDED TO THE RENT BOARD. THE RENT BOARD WILL NEED TO SET ASIDE DECISION ON REMAND AND CONDUCT FURTHER PROCEEDINGS CONSISTENT WITH THIS COURT'S ORDER. COUNSEL WERE INSTRUCTED TO MEET AND CONFER TO PREPARE A JOINT FORM OF ORDER, OTHERWISE, EACH PARTY IS TO SUBMIT THEIR OWN PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189

calendar.matters:
For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ Of Mandate;Table Of Authorities;
Off Calendar Jul-11-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Hindman of Zitrin and Mastomonaco, LLP, 354-2703, appearing for Real Party in Interest Sharon Shiu
Robert DeVries of DeVries and Gold, 546-5100, appearing for petitioner Thomas Brown

After a personal disclosure of the Court, this matter was argued. The Court granted Petitioner Thomas Brown's Petition for Peremptory Writ of Mandate. The matter is remanded to the Rent Board. The Rent Board will need to set aside decision on remand and conduct further proceedings consistent with this Court's order. Counsel were instructed to meet and confer to prepare a joint form of order, otherwise, each party is to submit their own proposed order to the Court. (301/PJB/DM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189
